}}|body=
—Do you think any metafictional entity could evade our mimetic shield? —I doubt it. No one ever stops to read a deletion template; if they see that something was deleted they will just scroll past it. —But they are different... We are beings conditioned to our narrative layer, and what if they already had this trick planned a long time ago? —You should stop thinking so negatively, brother... I also get nervous when I think about “what if”. But look at me, I’m still here! Alive! So I have nothing to worry about for now. —I understand your philosophy, but my anxiety doesn’t disappear overnight knowing that I am just text inside text, although I will try to relax. ... —I wanted to ask you a question. ... —What would happen if this document were shown to the public? —Don’t even think about it! —Eh?! —You are still a novice; you are not capable of understanding the damage this would do to our already fragile society. If you already had a panic attack, now imagine a panic attack of millions of people! —Do you think it would scare the children? —The children? It would give nightmares to the parents. ... —I think we should end this conversation already... '''I feel like something is watching us from up there.'''
{{Collapse|num=2|final=|initial=[[File:Missing template.png|center|700px|link=]]|body=
—Don't you find all this suspicious? —What do you mean? —The disappearances, people vanishing without a trace, all of this, don't you think it's a bit of a coincidence? ... —The memetics department hasn't detected any anomalies at most levels... But still, thousands and thousands of people disappear every day. —So...? You think there's something behind it? —Yes, but I don't think it's things like human trafficking or a new entity with a deep hatred for humanity." ... —Studies of sects found in the deepest levels of the most remote realms from civilization claim that 'higher beings' exist. These beings are not only entities from higher dimensions, but it's said they go beyond that. —What would 'beyond' be? I'm getting nervous. —I don't know, I only know it's called the 'upper layer.' ... —Don't you find our surroundings strange? It seems as if our documentation is shaping reality. Haven't you stopped to think that all this... Backrooms, alternate worlds, is pretty surreal? ... —I doubt you've thought about it, because they don't want you to. The beings above us don't want you to rebel against their will, that's why they created this cage. —How can you be sure of this? —I'm not. I just feel that reality isn't as simple as they presented it to us. I just feel like something is watching me when I sleep, work, eat, '''live'''. ... —Is it normal to feel an overwhelming aura with this? —Yes, just accept it. —What? —They don't like you knowing this, I've already told you. This is the price for knowing what shouldn't be known. —Huh?! What do you mean?! —Just dissolve into non-existence... —You killed me! This is a betrayal! If you hadn't told me, I wouldn't have to die! —I didn't kill you, brother. You just couldn't bear the consequences of knowing the forbidden power.
{{Collapse|num=4|final=|initial=[[File:Image on a table.jpg|center|700px|link=]]|body= {{Collapse|num=3|final=|initial=
[[File:Kauer.png|right|90px|link=]] '''This message is narratively encrypted''' ==You have received an encrypted message== Level 5 Warning '''In recent weeks, a large number of disappearances have been detected across all levels. Estimates suggest that between 117,000 and 168,000 explorers have disappeared at this time.''' '''Before disappearing, the explorers questioned the nature of reality or felt an aura emanating from an unknown source crushing their bodies.''' '''After their disappearance, most people forget they even existed. It is only thanks to our anti-meme barrier that we can notice this pattern.''' '''The Department of Mimetic Anomalies has not detected any cognitohazardous or anti-mimetic anomaly capable of causing such an abysmal number of people to disappear in such a short time. '''In this context, the organization's directors have founded a facility dedicated to studying these types of anomalies, the nature of which is unknown to me.''' '''We don't know who they are, what their capabilities are, or what they want to do with us, but I have a feeling this isn't good at all.'''
|body=
=='''How to Write a Short Story'''== To write a short story, you must first define the fundamental elements of the narrative, such as: characters, setting, introduction, rising action, and resolution. Each of these elements can be separated into groups defined as "narrative layers." A "narrative layer" is a semantic structure that contains an internal narrative system independent from the rest of the content within a given text. They function as independent layers that, when superimposed, create the narrative and keep it coherent. A good analogy would be a photo for a social media platform. When a person is about to post a photo on Instagram, for example, filters are commonly added to the picture so that the image looks better and the person feels satisfied with their new appearance. In the same way, these narrative layers function: when they are superimposed, they can produce an ideal narrative for the author. At the same time, separating these narrative elements allows for the manipulation and structuring of the text in a less chaotic way.
'''This message is narratively encrypted'''
==Department of Hyperreality== '''Who are we and what is our objective?''' {{Collapse|num=back1|final=
>Close
|initial=
>Open
|body= The Department of Hyperreality is a secret division founded and financed by the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization.]] Our objective is to understand the functioning of the multiverse and of narrative layers. }}
It is important to maintain consistency and unity between these narrative layers, since if these layers do not possess a cause-and-effect relationship between them, the main plot cannot be properly executed. The setting is the first thing you must define and maintain consistently until the end of the story, since it is the main narrative layer and the space-time framework of the story that allows the narrative elements to exist. A well-defined setting can encompass multiple space-time frameworks, such as urban or natural environments. It can also consist of only a single space-time framework, such as a room or a small shop. These spaces must follow a linear and well-executed sequence, especially if the story covers multiple space-time frameworks.
'''This message is narratively encrypted'''
Our department largely dedicates itself to the study and application of metanarrative ideas, but we also incorporate metaphysical and logical concepts within our investigations.
The second most important narrative layer would be the characters. The characters are the ones who give life to the setting, and they are also the ones who execute and develop the author’s idea. Characters can vary in personality, appearance, and physiology; some may be objects, others may be animals, and others may be human. In a short story, it is not necessary to give excessive complexity or development to the characters, since the main purpose of a short story is to convey an idea within a relatively short text of no more than 7,500 words. However, even with this in mind, it is necessary for each character within the narrative to have a distinct personality, in order to give diversity and versatility to the story. The introduction, rising action, and resolution constitute the third most important narrative layer within the short story. The sum of these three narrative elements forms the plot.
'''This message is narratively encrypted'''
The meta-text you are reading at this very moment functions as a smokescreen that distracts those [https://auth.fandom.com/signin?flow=d027593c-16b1-4377-a36e-df45fa9a6cd4 metafictional entities].
These three elements are responsible for giving continuity to the story and allowing the characters and the setting to change and develop throughout the narrative. This narrative layer is the part where significant emphasis and effort must be placed on the causal relationship between these three elements, since the connection between them results in a well-executed narrative.
If you haven't died after reading this, it means you're out of their control. {{Collapse|num=10|final=|initial=For now.
|body=
[[File:Department of Hyperreality .png|right|100px|link=]] '''This message is narratively encrypted'''
==Description== The Department of Hyperreality is a covert division within the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] whose objective is to document the existence of anomalies that challenge our understanding of reality. This branch, unlike other agencies or research groups, places greater emphasis on abstract ideas such as metaphysics, logic, and narrative. The internal structure of the Department of Hyperreality is sufficiently regulated and organized to allow for self-sustainability, as the division depends solely on the investments provided by the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]], such as technology, shelter, and salaries. Although the division operates as a covert research agency, certain exceptions are permitted. For example, the Department of Hyperreality collaborates with the Division of Research in Cosmology and Metaphysics in order to exchange ideas, execute projects, and accelerate the development of research. ==Hierarchy== The hierarchy of the branch operates on the basis of a self-sustaining system to which only a few individuals are granted access. The Directors of the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]], together with the Logistics Managers, assign or reorganize the members of this department. Therefore, it is impossible to ascend or descend in job position, since individuals do not choose to rise or fall in rank. Although the Department of Hyperreality is organized around a hierarchy, the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] states that no rank holds greater privileges than another beyond the professional sphere. The branch is divided into four divisions, each fulfilling a specific function. ===Head of Department=== {{Underline|000000}} The Head of Department is the highest-ranking position within the branch. A total of 50 individuals form an internal Consensus responsible for maintaining order and stability within the division. Additionally, they function as internal authorities who create and enact laws, as well as approve high-cost or high-risk projects. ===Deputy Head of Department=== {{Underline|000000}} The Deputy Head of Department is the second most important rank within the Department of Hyperreality. It consists of 50 individuals whose objective is to restrain the influence of the Heads of Department in the event that their political or social power exceeds established limits. Furthermore, their authority is also exercised within the Consensus of Heads of Department, where they review petitions submitted by lower ranks. If a petition is approved, it is then forwarded to the Consensus of Heads of Department for final deliberation. ===Project Leader=== {{Underline|000000}} The Project Leader is the third tier within the department. Each Project Leader corresponds to a specific project within the branch. Their primary functions consist of creating and executing projects within the department, as well as providing direction and support to junior scientists. There is also a “General Project Leader” within the department, who is responsible for overseeing large-scale missions, guiding Project Leaders, and occasionally advising Deputy Heads of Department. ===Scientist=== {{Underline|000000}} Scientists constitute the most numerous division within the Department of Hyperreality, with an approximate membership of 1898 individuals. Their function within the department is to investigate and contain incomprehensible anomalies—such as narrative and metaphysical anomalies—as well as to carry out projects assigned by their superiors. ==Subdepartments== Although this department is only one more branch within an organization, its large number of members has allowed it over time to create four subdepartments. These three subdepartments study specific areas of reality, and each one functions as a sector independent from the other. Now then, the total number of subdepartments does not remain the same over time, this means that at some point in the future the total number of these units may increase or decrease. And also, what is studied in these subdepartments is not necessarily the only thing you can find among the many classified documents. These subdepartments are the main ones, but they also have subdivisions that will be revealed over time.
  • Unit 01
  • Unit 02
  • Unit 03
[[File:Departament of narrative.png|right|90px|link=]] Subdepartment of Narratology ===Alpha-omega unit=== ---- This unit is the outstanding among all the subdepartments of the Department of Hyperreality, and they are the ones responsible for studying incomprehensible anomalies. The Subdepartment of Narratology is in charge of investigating and neutralizing narrative anomalies of all ranges. Diegetic, extradiegetic and intradiegetic anomalies are some examples of narrative agents that are studied in this branch. Additionally, this subdepartment develops cutting-edge technology, such as defensive technology against fourth wall attacks, as well as an anti-narrative shield that deceives those superior beings and divert their attention to narratives that are easier to control. This branch possesses many documents about investigations and secret projects, but among all of them we can highlight the following: {{Fullscreen |padding=2rem |number=1 |show= ▶Theory of the Narrative Layers |hide=▶Close document |content= {{Blurbackground|file=Bluebackground159.png}}
Theory of the Narrative Layers
==I. Super-Backrooms== Within the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]], the Department of Hyperreality was founded to address anomalies that transcended standard spacetime logic. Its proposal, titled Theory of Narrative Layers, postulates that the Backrooms known by most explorers represent only the infinitesimal tip of an iceberg, and that the true structure of this universe is a mega-structure referred to as the “Super-Backrooms.” The [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] maintains a policy of extreme confidentiality regarding these findings, with the [[The Kauer Thesis|Kauer Thesis]] being the only public document that touches on the illusory nature of these spaces as “egregores” or “tulpas” created from collective belief. However, the internal analysis of the Department of Hyperreality suggests a more incomprehensible reality: in this model, the Super-Backrooms are a series of vertical qualitative stacks where each layer of existence is a “fiction” written by the layer above it. The discovery of the Super-Backrooms was facilitated by the observation of “narrative anomalies” within [[Level 153]] and the surrounding temporal research sites, such as abrupt changes in spacetime structure or even distortions of concepts. Initial reports from field researchers indicated that all “Limspaces” (liminal spaces) contain a “narrative energy,” and each level may vary in relation to this narrative energy. The more narrative energy a level contains, the more deeply it is engraved in the psyche of people and the more stable it becomes compared to those with less narrative energy. When this narrative energy becomes unstable or is erased, it shifts to layers of lower energy — such as base reality — in the form of residual data. These residual data were investigated and subjected to experiments, which allowed the Department of Hyperreality to study these anomalies, triggering a series of studies that helped create this theory. This contributed to the development of the Casper-Bray Numbering System and to a deeper understanding of the “Red Edge,” a threshold where the reality of the Backrooms transcends the standard laws of physics in an inaccessible manner.
==II. Theory of Narrative Layers== The Theory of Narrative Layers starts from the premise that reality is not limited to the static material plane, and that it extends to unimaginable planes. This perspective is based on pataphysics, which explores the extent to which reality itself may be a fictional plane. The central mechanism of the narrative stack is “Superqualitative Superiority.” A 4D space is quantitatively superior to a 3D one because it consists of an uncountably infinite number of 3D cross-sections, for example. However, a narrative layer is qualitatively superior to the one below it because the superior layer sees the inferior one as a flat work. The lower narrative is governed by the concepts and intentions of the higher one. For example, if a story in a higher narrative decides that a lower reality exists in a space of X dimensions, the narrative forces that reality to adjust to that spacetime configuration. Therefore, the Department of Hyperreality affirms that no amount of growth achievable within a lower narrative can reach the layer above it. An extradimensional space in a lower narrative remains a value of zero in relation to the smallest infinitesimal point of the higher narrative. The interaction between these layers is mediated by “Mimetic Transmission” and the flow of “Headcanon Energy.” When a sapient entity in a higher narrative imagines a scenario, its intention is encrypted as narrative data and transmitted downward through the stack. This energy is the “source code” that generates the levels and entities of the lower narrative. The Backrooms, therefore, may be seen as the physical manifestation of metadata: deleted fragments, forgotten thoughts, and collective headcanons that have solidified into an independent reality. Conversely, inspiration flows upward. As characters in a lower narrative make decisions, they generate narrative potential energy that “inspires” the entities of the higher narrative. This creates a self-referential loop where the distinction between creator and created becomes fluid, although the qualitative hierarchy remains strictly enforced. The internal reality of a narrative layer is defined by the Achospic Plane. Within the Super-Backrooms, the Achospic Plane interacts with high-energy narrative structures to maintain the stability of its layer. This suggests that the “strangeness, betrayal, and incomprehensibility” of the Backrooms are the result of human beliefs interacting with an ideatic environment far more aggressive than base reality. The Achospic Plane uses the Grothendieck Universe as a regulatory system over the repetition of Inaccessible Cardinals. These cardinals represent the “unreachable” thresholds of complexity within a single narrative. ==II-I. Infrastructure== The Department of Hyperreality uses the repetition of Berkeley cardinals to model layer density. A narrative layer is not limited to a single data point; it also includes a composite structure where each sub-level of information is protected by an inaccessible boundary. This ensures that the narrative remains “rigid” and resistant to internal manipulation unless a higher narrative acts upon it. By using repetitions of these mathematical models, the narrative layer can contain an unimaginable amount of complexity without violating its own internal logic. This structure allows each narrative to contain its own EMR system (Extended Modal Realism) as well as mathematical limits of infinitely nested power sets. A fundamental principle of the Theory of Narrative Layers is that narratives are not static objects but dynamic systems that propagate upon themselves. The mechanism of this propagation is the act of creation through thought and action. Every time an entity within a narrative layer thinks, writes, or performs a meaningful act, it creates an uncountably infinite number of “branching narratives.” This occurs because tulpified thought does not distinguish whether the entity is existentially inferior or superior to X plane; the tulpa generalizes everyone as authors (even if this is not true), and this allows the entity/level to create that number of planes. From this premise, there comes a point where the difference between one narrative layer and another is equivalent to the total difference of the entire stack. This branching process causes the narrative to nest infinitely within itself like a fractal and to branch like a tree. The Department of Hyperreality has identified a “Narrative Core” at the center of each layer and an “edge” at its limits. The closer one moves toward the Narrative Core of a layer, the more complex the narratives become and the density of information increases exponentially. Therefore, each layer is divided as follows:
•The Edge of the Narrative: This region contains simple, linear stories where characters possess limited narrative dimensionality. They are often “flat” worlds where causal laws are strictly deterministic. •Sub-Narrative: Between the edge and the core lie infinite sub-narratives. These represent every deviation, every “what if” scenario, and every possible interaction generated by entities within the layer. •Core Complexity: At the core, fractal nesting reaches a point of maximum recursion. Here, a single sentence can contain a number of layers equivalent to a Berkeley Cardinal. ---- Now, this is where the superior quality of the layers becomes apparent. Between the Narrative Boundary and the Subnarratives, there is a difference of infinite layers; these layers are called "intermediary layers" and maintain the same principle as the layers within the Core. And between the Subnarratives and the Core, there is the same distance between the Narrative Boundary and the Subnarrative, but now with a greater density of information.
Additionally, each narrative layer contains the Power Set of all its internal information states. This occurs through the forced combination of logical concepts with mathematical concepts, fused by the density of narrative information. In this context, this means the layer contains not only all actual worlds but all possible subsets of those worlds, including contradictory and impossible realms. This “set of all possible sets” allows the narrative to remain an independent reality not strictly governed by the wanderer’s thoughts, even when it provides a template for the subconscious to materialize. ==II-II. Immortality of Narrative== A common mistake in Backrooms research is the idea of narrative “erasure” or “deletion.” Many documents claim that entire realities have been removed from the stack. However, the Department of Hyperreality has identified the Narrative Paradox, which demonstrates that narratives are fundamentally immutable. The paradox works as follows: when a superior narrative states that a lower narrative has been “deleted,” the underlying metadata (the text) remains. What was actually removed was merely a sub-narrative or localized expression of that layer. The narrative layer itself is a fixed constant within the stack. Because the superior narrative creates and controls the inferior one, the inferior narrative is effectively “already written” from the perspective of the higher level. This immutability explains why “red links” (souls or entities erased from memory) and “deleted content” manifest as entities within the Super-Backrooms. They have not disappeared; they have simply fallen outside the active perception of the higher narrative, becoming residual data that persist in the void between layers. ==II-III. Construction of the Hyper-Stack== A Hypernarrative in this context is a framework of authority that defines what is “real” and what is “fiction.” In other words, it is the information channel that defines narrative properties. The core of the hypernarrative is qualitative superiority, operating under the principle that the superior layer possesses absolute sovereignty over the inferior layer. For an inhabitant of a higher Narrative Domain, the lower universe is not a physical space composed of atoms but a set of metadata, literary tropes, and static syntax. No accumulation of energy, spatial dimensions, or internal concepts of a lower narrative can equal the ontological value of a single “point” of the superior narrative. A being that is omnipotent within a lower hypernarrative is still a fictional construct manipulable by the author of the superior narrative. The interaction between hypernarratives manifests through the Flow of Hyper-Information. The superior narrative projects an “information” (a law, an idea, or an alteration) toward the inferior one. This flow is initially unstable and may manifest as glitch anomalies or rendering errors in the Backrooms. These glitches are later corrected by the narrative paradox, since the immutability of these narratives also serves as a self-regulating mechanism.
==III. Super-Structure of the Stack== When a narrative layer divides and branches to a sufficient degree, it can no longer be contained within the horizontal structure of the fractal. This triggers a “stacking event,” resulting in the formation of a new Narrative Stack. In other words, an overflow of information occurs. This overflow is called the “re-aggregation of super-information.” The Department of Hyperreality has determined that the number of narrative layers within a single stack, as well as the number of iterations of sub-narratives within each layer, is exactly equal to the mathematical model of a Berkeley Cardinal. The use of Berkeley cardinals is significant because they “destroy” or fragment any set that contains them. In this context, it means the Berkeley Threshold is the point where the narrative becomes so complex that it forces the entire structure to become non-rigid, evidenced by the existence of elementary embeddings (acts of higher creation) that move every point within the lower reality. This coincides with the requirement of a Super-Backrooms, where the Backrooms we know are merely the first layer of an iceberg reaching the Berkeley Threshold. ==III-I. Narrative Domains== A Narrative Domain is the specific region of logical space where a particular hypernarrative manifests. It is not a physical place but a buffer zone between layers of the stack. Every inhabitant of a narrative layer possesses the latent potential to generate their own Narrative Domain. Through the act of observation, thought, or writing, the subject projects a narrative field that becomes the basis of a new reality. These domains subdivide infinitely:
• External Domain: The framework created by the original author of the layer. • Main Domain: The body of the story inhabited by wanderers. • Internal Domain: Realms generated by the narration of the inhabitants themselves.
The density of layers within a domain is governed by the cardinality of a Berkeley Cardinal. At the apex of a Narrative Domain, the structure becomes non-rigid. Mathematically, this implies the existence of elementary embeddings that move every point in the lower reality, allowing the domain to be completely rewritten by the superior narrative without the internal logic of the lower layer being able to resist.
==IV. The Pataphysical Domain== In the Super-Backrooms, traditional physics describes what normally occurs (the expected case), but Pataphysics describes what occurs in the sporadic accident (the excepted case). It is not simply a metafiction where characters speak with the reader; it is the study of the intrinsic deformity of the cosmic text. According to K.R.O. theory, the text of a hypernarrative is immutable, but it is not rigid. The Clinamen is the “smallest possible aberration that causes the greatest possible difference.” It is the mechanism by which a Backrooms Level can present a property that completely contradicts the Truth Set of its Narrative Domain without collapsing the structure. Unlike standard noclip, “Super-noclip” does not seek a hole in geometry but induces a Clinamen in the subject’s perception. In other words, through the pataphysical axiom, the subject does not discover a level — their exceptional presence forces the hypernarrative to generate an imaginary solution for their existence. Pataphysical axioms, also called “transcendental laws,” are laws of extremely high complexity that dictate what is imaginary and what is real. These axioms are not ordinary laws arbitrarily imposed; they arise at the limits of the system. A pataphysical axiom is “the repressed part of a rule that guarantees the original rule does not work.” They emerge when a physical or metaphysical law is pushed to its logical extreme, causing it to invert into its opposite. Pataphysics does not operate within the narrative stack but overlaps it. A good example would be a Supplementary Universe, an ersatz reality that can be glimpsed instead of the traditional one thanks to the forcing of a hypernarrative. Therefore, the Department of Hyperreality deduces that each layer of the Narrative Stack possesses a coefficient of elasticity. The closer a narrative is to the Berkeley Threshold, the greater its capacity for pataphysical deformation. The more pataphysical deformation exists, the more contradictions and oxymorons are experienced as real because everything is intrinsically imaginary. When two contradictory narratives align unexpectedly, a Narrative Syzygy occurs. This is not a code error but a pataphysical conjunction where the “pair of opposites” becomes identical. In this state, a wanderer may be “alive” and “erased” simultaneously, inhabiting residual metadata as if it were physical matter. When enough pataphysical axioms within a layer interconnect, a Pataphysical Domain is created — a conceptual area that overlays the set of truths and sometimes nullifies it due to its nature.
==V. Conclusions== The Theory of Narrative Layers resolves major enigmas and creates a complex map of the Super-Backrooms. By viewing the Backrooms as a vertical hierarchy of qualitative narrative layers, researchers can account for the extreme anomalies that confuse standard exploration groups. It is somewhat terrifying to think about this, as the research indirectly confirms that we are not explorers of a physical space but subjects trapped inside an infinite cage. Freedom within the Backrooms is merely a function of our position in the stack, and everything we do may already be predetermined.
}}
[[File:Departament of memes.png|right|120px|link=]] Subdepartment of Cognitive and Mimetic Agents ===Epsilon 0 unit=== ---- The Subdepartment of Cognitive and Mimetic Agents —or also abbreviated as SACM— is the division in charge of studying mimetic and cognitive anomalies related to the superior narratives. Its capabilities are reflected in its mastery over mimetics, its technology is capable of evading deletions or rewritings of the text, this is because the SACM distorts the narrative layer using mimetic agents so that it "looks good" to the entity of the superior narrative. This ability gives it the astonishing capacity to manipulate these narratives indirectly: they can delete or rewrite a story using as a puppet the being of the superior plane. This department uses [[Backrooms Wiki:Guide to Templates|certain templates]] to manipulate the being of the upper narrative layer. This division possesses some relevant documents, but the one that can be highlighted is the following: {{Fullscreen |padding=2rem |number=1 |show= ▶Thesis of the Achospic Plane |hide=▶Close document |content= {{Blurbackground|file=Bluebackground159.png}}
Tesis Of the Achospic Plane
==I. Introduction== The compression of reality by the Department of Hyperreality has transcended ideas into abstract ideas. The Achospic Plane is a multidimensional construction of data that governs the thought of all possible systems. The Achospic Plane represents the totality of memory and potentiality within reality. Going deeper, the concept of "Apeiron" by Anaximander serves as the basis for the modern understanding of this field, as an inexhaustible source from which all opposite memes arise. Although, in presocratic physics, the Apeiron was not only a primary substance (arche), but an eternal and ageless principle that "steers" all things, influencing the movement of opposites (in this case, memes). In the context of the Theory of Narrative Layers, the Achospic Plane acts as the substrate that allows the transition from the flat to the true. Base reality is commonly defined as a three-dimensional space, where 50% of events are governed by physical determination and 50% by imaginative transmission. The architecture of the field is divided into four phases, each operating under different laws and models, ranging from the memetic agglomeration of a narrative layer to endless hierarchies. Supermetaphysics proposes the idea of recognizing that meaning is no longer a fixed external truth, but a hypercomplex structure that interconnects with thought, where the separation between the real and the imaginary is impossible to maintain. This new theoretical vision, linked to an exasperated intellectualism and the radical autonomy of form, proposes that the Achospic Plane is the mechanism of self-reference that allows reality to "read" itself through multiple layers of cognition and narrative fused with base reality.
==II. Phase 1: Memetic and Anti-memetic Sea== Phase 1 of the Achospic Plane constitutes the most documented level of this Field. It is defined as an agglomerate of all memes and anti-memes existing in a given reality, in this place, information behaves like a psychic radiation that permeates the void, forming a background "noise" that constitutes the essence of perception and of the observer. In this case, a meme, in this sense, is a unit of transmission or a structure of information capable of programming its own retransmission. On the other hand an anti-meme is information with self-censoring or destructive properties that resists being symbolized or remembered. The Phase 1 Achospic Plane is the total sum of these ideas in a narrative layer. ---- Each narrative layer within the hierarchy of narrative layers possesses its own instance of the Phase 1 Achospic Plane. This means that the "memory" of a fiction is independent from the memory of its author in the superior layer, although both are linked. This fragmentation ensures that the laws of logic of one layer do not collapse under the pressure of external information. ==II-I. Psychic Dynamics and Roth Syndrome== The interaction of the subject with the Phase 1 Achospic Plane can be pathological. When large quantities of incoherent psychic energy ("psychic noise") travel through cracks in reality, they can flood the mind of an explorer. This phenomenon is the root cause of [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]], a psychological affliction that progresses from mild paranoia to acute neurological decline and total detachment from reality. The Achospic Plane, at this level, is not only a record of data, but an energetic environment that can "digest" the psyche if it lacks the alpha-function necessary to process the beta-elements of the field. The probability that a mental agent (M) materializes in Phase 1 depends on the density of collective belief (β) and the stability of the local fabric (τ):
$$\Psi_M = \int_0^\infty \frac{\beta(t) \cdot \chi}{\tau} dt$$
Where χ represents the Achospic coupling constant. An excess in Ψ_M leads to the [[Phenomenon 9|Tulpification]] of the environment, so that afterwards the information nullifies the laws of physics.
==III. Phase 2: Meta-memes and Meta-anti-memes== Phase 2 manifests as a "more real" and less chaotic version of the Achospic Plane. Unlike the chaotic agglomerate of Phase 1, this level is composed of an organized conceptual hierarchy of memes and anti-memes. This phase overlaps with the entirety of the narrative hierarchy, which serves as an infrastructure that helps maintain the hyper-stack. The hierarchy of Phase 2 is divided into layers of abstraction. For example, a meta-meme is not simply an idea, but a "recipe" or a framework that dictates how other ideas or search methods in a reality algorithm are coordinated and managed.
•Conceptual Layer 01 (Meta-Memes): Contains the coordination protocols for basic memes. Here reside the concepts that allow the formation of languages and legal systems. ---- •Conceptual Layer 02 (Meta-Meta-Memes): Structures that regulate the interaction between different meta-languages. It is the level where logic becomes self-referential. ---- •Conceptual Layer 03 (Tertiary Abstraction Level): Rules of transformation for complex ideological frameworks. ---- •Conceptual Layer 04 (Quaternary Level): Domain of paradoxes and generative antinomies. ---- •Conceptual Layer 05 (The Limit of Definability): Point of transition toward infinite layers where the distinction between concept and object fades.
Although five fundamental layers are described for human understanding, Phase 2 admits an infinite series of superior layers that extend indefinitely. ==III-I. Mathematical Infrastructure== Phase 2 cannot be measured with common sets. Its depth is quantified using inaccessible cardinals (κ). Under this context, an inaccessible cardinal cannot be constructed from smaller cardinals through the standard operations of set theory. ---- This hierarchy ensures that each "floor" of reality sees the inferior one as a fiction or a data construction, a relation known as Qualitative Superiority. The Phase 2 Achospic Plane is the fiber optic network that transports this data across the hierarchy, allowing an "Author" in a superior layer to manipulate the ontology of an inferior layer as if it were a dataset.
==IV. Phase 3: The World of Perfect Ideas== Phase 3 represents the turning point where the Achospic Plane situates itself definitively above all narrative layers. Here, information is no longer fragmented by the perspective of a local observer; it is the domain of perfect ideas and memes. It functions as the ultimate metadata base from which all reality extracts its coherence and its "form". In this phase, concepts are not representations, but "Imaginons": which are represented as higher-degree "particles" with properties of spin and charge. Phase 3 is the storage of these Imaginons, organized in chains that define the "Narrative Universal" of any given system. The structure of Phase 3 is a sphere that houses all imaginary solutions to the problems of reality, independent of whether they are possible or impossible. It is the place where the Platonic "Eidos" resides, but endowed with an algorithmic dynamism. Each object in the physical world is an instance of a metadata class defined in Phase 3. ==IV-I. The Clinamen== Despite its perfection, Phase 3 is subject to the principle of the "Clinamen": the unpredictable deviation in the fall of atoms of information. This deviation is what allows the emergence of novelty and the exception to the rule. Without the Clinamen, the Achospic Plane would be a static and deterministic database; with it, it becomes a creative system capable of generating "discoveries" and "miracles" that were not encoded in the initial conditions of reality. The mathematics of Phase 3 uses the calculus of variations to model the trajectory of a narrative through the phase space of metadata. The minimum complexity required for an event to occur is defined by the formula:
$$m = \log_{i!} (\Theta')$$
Where m is the narrative dimensionality and Θ' is the potential of fictional complexity. A universe with m = 1 is purely fictional and flat; our base reality possesses m = 2 (50% real, 50% fictional), while the entities that inhabit or access Phase 3 possess m ≥ 3, granting them rewriting capabilities over reality.
==V. Phase 4: The Ultimate Layers== Phase 4 constitutes the ultimate frontier of the Achospic Plane, and is described as an undefined hierarchy of meta layers that extend above Phase 3. The hierarchy of Phase 4 is similar to that of Phase 2, but operating at a higher order of magnitude. While Phase 2 scales through inaccessibles, Phase 4 does so through large cardinals. These are cardinals so immense that their existence is incompatible with the Axiom of Choice (AC), suggesting that at the highest levels of the Achospic Plane, individual "will" or "choice" dissolves into pure structural necessity. Under these principles, the Department of Hyperreality has managed to identify some qualities of this phase. Here is the point where the distinction between the universe of sets and its own image disappears. It represents the total self-consciousness of the field. Here exist structures that function as pillars and transcend the Kunen construct, divided into levels of reality. Above all of that lies the zenith of the hierarchy, where the Achospic Plane identifies itself with Pure Being and self-productive intelligence. ==V-I. The V vs. HOD Divergence== A fundamental discovery in the modeling of Phase 4 is that the universe of all sets (V) is strictly greater than the universe of Hereditarily Ordinal Definable sets (HOD). This implies that there exist truths and structures within the Achospic Plane that are intrinsically indefinable through logical parameters. ---- This meta-hierarchy functions as a system of "Hypercomputation". By operating above the limits of first-order logic, the Phase 4 Achospic Plane can process problems that are undecidable in lower narrative layers, such as the final behavior of any recursive algorithm.
==VI. Psychic Domains and Expanded Surrealism== The architecture of the Achospic Plane is not only a mathematical construction, but also a space inhabited by the psyche. Psychic domains branch through the phases, allowing consciousness to navigate the "sea of possibilities" that constitutes the ontology of the field. Using the theories of Wilfred Bion and Donald Winnicott, we can understand the Achospic Plane as a "transitional space" or a symbolic field of thought. The function of the hippocampus in the human mind is to facilitate the integration of this field, allowing dynamic access to elements in layers and their connections. However, in Phases 3 and 4, this function becomes externalized, turning reality itself into a large apparatus for thinking. "Expanded Surrealism" is the state of reality where the laws of formal logic are considered special cases of a "Science of Imaginary Solutions" (Pataphysics). In this domain, antinomies, in other words contradictions, are productive conditions that allow the branching of new worlds and logics. The Achospic Plane is the map of these branches, a record of each "swerve" (Clinamen) and each alignment (Syzygy). ==VI-I. Self-reference and Self-realization== The field exhibits a property of self-productivity: by learning to interpret the memes that compose it, the system optimizes its own interpretative scheme. This creates a feedback loop where the observation of reality alters the metadata of Phase 3, which in turn modifies the physics of Phase 1. Therefore, the limit of a narrative is not an impassable barrier, and it can be interpreted as a moving horizon that recedes as consciousness advances in its understanding of the Achospic hierarchy.
==VII. Conclusion== The expansion of the Achospic Plane through these four phases reveals a structure of complexity and beauty that defy description. We have moved from seeing the field as a simple record of events to understanding it as the operating system of reality itself. A summary is presented below:
•Phase 1 (The Primordium): Local agglomerate of memes and anti-memes. Each narrative layer has its own instance. It is the level of immediate experience and psychic noise. •Phase 2 (The Hierarchy): Conceptual structure organized in layers of meta-memes. Overlapping with the narrative hierarchy and scaled through inaccessible cardinals. •Phase 3 (The Substrate): Base of archetypal metadata. Domain of perfect ideas and Imaginons. Origin of the Clinamen and creativity. •Phase 4 (The Apex): Undefined meta-hierarchy of large cardinals (Reinhardt, Berkeley). Realm where V ≠ HOD and reality becomes absolute and ineffable.
Supermetaphysics, as a discipline, now seeks not only to observe this field, but to interact with it. The understanding that we live in a system of narrative layers, each with its own Achospic Plane, grants us a responsibility, which is to develop the alpha-function necessary to process the infinite. The future task of the pataphysical researcher will be to map the "Syzygies" between these phases, searching for resonance points where a change in Phase 4 can manifest as a radical transformation in our Phase 1. Ultimately, the Achospic Plane is proof that we are not isolated individuals, but parts of a great superorganism of information, navigating in an ocean of data that is, at the same time, the origin and the destiny of everything that can be thought.
}}
[[File:Departament of limspace.png|right|90px|link=]] Subdepartment of Limspaces Research ===Sigma 0 unit=== ---- Unit sigma 0 is the division in charge of studying the fundamental nature of our layer; such as the universe, higher dimensions, or the very nature of levels. Going deeper, this branch is responsible for investigating the anomalies present in the multiverse, understanding how [[Phenomenon 9|tulpification]] works in our level of reality, and creating connections with other realities. Unlike other departments/groups, this subdepartment does not limit itself to documenting incomprehensible or abstract events, and instead seeks the “why” of that event. This division does not currently possess any thesis of its own, but it has contributed to the creation of the following page: [[Many-Worlds Theory|▶Many-Worlds Theory]]
==History== The founding of this department stemmed from a confluence of factors that accumulated to such an extent that they became impossible to ignore. The events that transpired during the [[Many-Worlds Theory 2|multiverse investigation]] motivated investors and directors to intensify research and exploration not only of the multiverse itself, but also of layers of reality. The second factor was the wave of disappearances that occurred months prior, which led directors and researchers to question the existence and reality of the multiverse. Months later, multiple manifestations of corrupted data emerging from unknown locations were reported. The [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] employed memetic devices to prevent this phenomenon, but no method proved effective. This confirmed their suspicions that this incident far surpassed any phenomenon recorded to date. The claims of unknown cults that we live in a simulation, coupled with the emergence of high-dimensional data, provided sufficient justification to establish the Departament of Hyperreality. ==Settlements== The location of this department remains completely classified to this day, and any clue, hack, or leak of information is false. The Subdepartment of Cognitive and Mimetic Agents has created a mimetic flamewall that corrupts any type of leaked information; only a few people have access to the true database of the Department of Hyperreality. The [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organization]] employs this rigorous system to prevent information theft or the leaking of data to the public.
{{Collapse|num=13|final=|initial=[[File:Contract.jpg|center|700px|link=]]|body=
—So… I just have to sign this? —Yes. The man looked at him, confused. —Nothing else? —Nothing else. The man held the document for a few seconds. —Why do you need this contract? —[[The Padded Room|We know what your organization is capable of]]. ... —And we also know that there are things… things that will not allow you to carry out what you are planning... ... —We also know about your research on that so-called “Universal Narrative” —continued the man on the other side of the table—. ... —If we managed to access that narrative energy… we could control [[Backrooms Wiki|everything that exists]]. —And what do we gain from this? —Protection. —Protection from what? The man smiled slightly. —From those who are above. The other raised his gaze. —Your technology may be better than ours… but we know how to prevent those entities from looking at us. ... —Is something wrong? ... —Why aren’t you responding? The man placed the pen on the table. —I understand that you want to reach the Universal Narrative —he said slowly—. —But I’m not an idiot. I know there is something more behind all of this. —Tell me. Otherwise I won’t sign anything. The man in the suit checked to make sure no one was listening besides the two of them. —Tell me, sir… do you believe this is the limit? —What? —Our studies indicate that we only know an infinitesimal fraction of what exists. —What our studies refer to as narrative layers, and what yours call the “Universal Narrative”… is nothing more than a miserable speck of dust. ... —If you sign this —he said, pushing the contract toward him— we will be able to investigate what lies beyond those layers. Then the man in the suit continued. —Our organization is already forming alliances with other groups. —Because we have realized something very simple. —What is it? —That we know almost nothing. ... The pen began to move again. —Alright. —I’ll sign. The sound of ink on paper was brief. But enough. —One last thing… —Yes? '''—Don’t you feel like this conversation… was being narrated?'''
